core_mechanism: destructive interference between two coupled damped resonators (a strongly-radiating lossy mode and a weakly-coupled low-loss mode) that opens a narrow transparency window with steep normal dispersion and large group delay at a local minimum of loss
brief_reason: The abstract reports strong dispersion at a local minimum of losses and considerable pulse delay described as an analog of electromagnetically induced transparency, which is the signature of two interfering coupled resonances producing a slow-light transparency window.
